ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Milo Dinosaur. FOODON_03301099 (chocolate malt beverage) is the closest defensible parent category.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKK (chocolate-flavoured milk-based drink) is the closest defensible parent. No specific Milo Dinosaur code exists in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226365003 (chocolate drink) is the closest defensible parent. No specific Milo Dinosaur term exists.

serving.description: 1 large cup (approximately 500 ml / 500 g including ice)
serving.natural_language_portion: One large cup of Ice Milo Dinosaur, approximately 500 ml, served in a tall cup with ice and a generous heap of undissolved Milo powder on top.

nutrient_source.title: Milo (chocolate malt beverage, prepared with whole milk and condensed milk) — adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy and Nestlé Milo product nutrition panel
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service / Nestlé Milo Singapore product label (used for Milo powder composition cross-reference only)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for Ice Milo Dinosaur (Large) exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Values are estimated from: (1) USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for 'Cocoa mix, powder, prepared with whole milk' (FDC ID 169594) as the base beverage matrix; (2) Nestlé Milo Singapore product nutrition panel for Milo powder composition (energy, carbohydrate, sugar, protein, fat per 100g powder); (3) standard Singapore hawker preparation ratios (approximately 3 heaped teaspoons Milo powder per 200 ml, condensed milk ~20 ml, evaporated milk ~30 ml, ice ~200 g, extra Milo powder topping ~10 g). The large-cup serving is estimated at 500 g total weight. Per-100g values are derived from this composite estimate. Confidence is medium due to estimation from components.

gi_source.title: Milo (chocolate malt beverage drink, made up as directed) — GI value from University of Sydney GI Database; analogous estimate applied to Ice Milo Dinosaur preparation
gi_source.source_match_notes: The University of Sydney GI Database lists Milo (chocolate malt beverage, made up as directed with water) with a GI of approximately 55 (low). Ice Milo Dinosaur is prepared with Milo powder, condensed milk, evaporated milk, and ice, with an additional Milo powder topping. The condensed milk and extra powder increase the sugar load relative to the standard preparation. GI is estimated at 55 (low) based on the Milo beverage GI anchor. Glycemic load per 500 g serving = (55 × 59.0 g available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = 32.45.

hypertension.key_factor: Moderate sodium content (~210 mg per large serving)
hypertension.guidance: A single large serving of Ice Milo Dinosaur contributes approximately 210 mg sodium, which is within acceptable limits for most hypertensive individuals, but the high sugar content and condensed milk base warrant moderation as part of an overall dietary pattern.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High sugar load and high glycemic load per serving, despite low glycemic index
type2_diabetes.guidance: Ice Milo Dinosaur (Large) delivers approximately 59 g of available carbohydrate per serving. While its estimated glycemic index is 55 (low), the high carbohydrate content results in a very high glycemic load of ~32.45 per serving, driven by condensed milk and Milo powder; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid or strictly limit this beverage and consider unsweetened alternatives.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from condensed and evaporated milk, and high added sugar content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The condensed milk and evaporated milk in Ice Milo Dinosaur contribute saturated fat (~6 g per large serving) and high added sugars (~52.5 g per large serving), both of which are associated with adverse cardiovascular outcomes and should be limited in individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Ice Milo Dinosaur (Large) is a popular Singaporean cold beverage made from Milo, a chocolate malt drink powder produced by Nestlé, mixed with condensed milk, evaporated milk, and ice. The defining feature of the 'Dinosaur' variant is a generous heap of undissolved Milo powder piled on top of the drink, creating a visually distinctive and texturally layered experience where the dry powder gradually dissolves as the drinker stirs or sips. The large serving is typically around 500 ml. The drink is sweet, rich, and chocolatey with a malty undertone, and the extra powder topping adds a slightly gritty, intensely malty flavour burst. It is widely available at Singaporean kopitiams (traditional coffee shops), hawker centres, and café chains such as Toast Box. Nutritionally, it is a high-sugar, moderate-energy beverage with approximately 360 kcal per large serving, 59 g of carbohydrate, and 10 g of fat, primarily from dairy. While its glycemic index is estimated at 55 (low), the high carbohydrate content results in a very high glycemic load (~32.45 per serving), making it a concern for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or metabolic syndrome. It provides modest amounts of calcium, iron, and B vitamins from the Milo powder fortification. The drink is vegetarian and halal-certified (Milo is halal-certified in Singapore) but contains milk and gluten (from barley malt in Milo). It is not suitable for vegans or those with lactose intolerance or gluten sensitivity.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for Ice Milo Dinosaur (Large) exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy cocoa malt beverage data and Nestlé Milo product nutrition panel, combined with standard Singapore kopitiam preparation ratios. GI is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database Milo beverage entry. Confidence is medium due to component-based estimation rather than direct laboratory analysis of the finished dish.
notes: 1. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy cocoa malt beverage source or the Nestlé Milo product panel. 2. Vitamin E is -1 as it is not reported in the primary source data used for this estimation. 3. Selenium is -1 as it is not reported in the primary source data. 4. Trans fat is 0.0; confirmed negligible/trace in dairy-based beverages at this preparation level per USDA SR Legacy data. 5. Dietary fibre is 0.2 g/100g, reflecting trace fibre from Milo powder (cocoa solids); this is a low but non-zero value consistent with the source. 6. The large serving size of 500 g is an estimate based on standard Singapore kopitiam large cup volumes; actual restaurant serving sizes may vary. 7. The GI of 55 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database Milo beverage entry; no direct GI measurement for Ice Milo Dinosaur with condensed milk and extra powder topping has been published. 8. Allergen information is based on the Nestlé Milo Singapore product label (milk, gluten from barley malt, soy). 9. The food is classified as 'processed' due to the use of commercially manufactured Milo powder, condensed milk, and evaporated milk. 10. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g are estimated from USDA SR Legacy dairy and cocoa malt beverage fatty acid profiles.
